target.name: Ephos
target.founder: Andrea Rocchetto, Francesco Ceccarelli, Ciro Pentangelo

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ephos is a seed-stage semiconductor company that has raised €41.5M in EU funding plus $8.5M in seed capital. The valuation universe consists of 3 public semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private comparables including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor—a total of 11 data points across the Chips & Semiconductors sector.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist, filtering for identical stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier yielded fewer than the required 3 datapoints per bucket. Public-comp anchoring, using the semiconductor sector P/S median of 20.3x across the three public tickers, could not be executed because Ephos's revenue is not publicly disclosed—standard practice for private companies. The absence of disclosed revenue eliminates the denominator required for revenue-multiple application.

Given the inapplicability of both primary methods, no quantitative valuation range can be derived from the available data. The analysis instead identifies key uncertainties blocking valuation: revenue run-rate and unit economics remain unreported. Future valuation work would require either Ephos disclosing revenue (enabling public-comp anchoring) or identifying a sufficiently dense peer set of private semiconductors at identical stage with available pricing data (enabling peer-median triangulation). Without these inputs, no sober estimate is supportable.
